title: For Loops Practice
instructions: Practice for loops, range(), enumerate(), and zip(). Write functions that iterate over sequences effectively.
hints: Use accumulator pattern to sum numbers
Use counter pattern to count items
insert(0, item) adds item at beginning of list
zip() pairs corresponding elements from two lists

testCases.description: sum_numbers should return the sum of a list of positive integers
sum_numbers should return 0 for an empty list
count_evens should count even numbers in a mixed list
count_evens should return 0 when no even numbers exist
reverse_list should return items in reverse order
create_pairs should pair corresponding elements using zip
testCases.validation: assert create_pairs(['a', 'b', 'c'], [1, 2, 3]) == [('a', 1), ('b', 2), ('c', 3)], 'create_pairs should pair elements correctly'

description: Practice using for loops to iterate over sequences in Python. Implement functions using the accumulator pattern, counter pattern, insert operations, and the zip() built-in to pair elements from multiple lists. These are fundamental iteration techniques you'll use constantly in Python programming.
